exercises.name: Band Anchored Tricep Kickback
exercises.description:
1. Start by securing a band to an anchor point at about chest height. 

2. Take the handles in both hands by your sides. 

3. Hinge at the hips, so your torso comes forward to near parallel with the floor. 

4. While keeping your elbows by your sides, drive your hands backwards and away from the anchor point until your arm are fully straightened. 

5. Slowly return to the start position and repeat.

exercises.name: Band Anchored Tricep Pushdown
exercises.description:
1. Start by securing a band to an anchor point above head height. 

2. Grasp the handles in both hands, keeping your elbows by your sides. 

3. Extend your hands down towards the floor, while keeping your elbows in place. 

4. Slowly return to the start position and repeat.

exercises.name: Band Assisted Chin Up
exercises.description:
1.Grab a pull up bar, palms facing you, arms and legs straightened making your body hang, foot is supported by a resistance band, the other foot wrapped around the other.

2.Pull your body upwards until your chin is just over the bar.

3.Lower your body returning to starting position.

4.Repeat.

exercises.name: Band Assisted Mid Grip Pull Up
exercises.alternateName: Band Assisted Pull Up Mid Grip
exercises.description:
1.Using close grip, grab a pull up bar. Palms facing away from you, arms and legs straightened making your body hang, the foot is supported by a resistance band, the other foot wrapped around the other.

2.Pull your body upwards until your chin is just over the bar.

3.Lower your body returning to starting position.

4.Repeat.

exercises.name: Band Assisted Parallel Grip Pull Up
exercises.description:
1.Grab a pull up bar in an upward fist position. Arms and legs straightened making your body hang, the foot is supported by a resistance band, the other foot wrapped around the other.

2.Pull your body upwards until your chin is just over the bar.

3.Lower your body returning to starting position.

4.Repeat.

exercises.name: Band Assisted Wide Grip Pull Up
exercises.alternateName: Resistance Band Assisted Pull Up Open Grip
exercises.description:
1.Using wide grip, grab a pull up bar. Palms facing away from you, arms and legs straightened making your body hang, the foot is supported by a resistance band, the other foot wrapped around the other.

2.Pull your body upwards until your chin is just over the bar.

3.Lower your body returning to starting position.

4.Repeat.

exercises.name: Band Bar Muscle Up
exercises.description:
1. Start by hanging on a bar with hands at shoulder-width, with a band secured to the bar wrapped below your foot for assistance. 

2. Slightly lean back as you pull the bar towards your torso, bringing yourself as high as possible. 

3. Once the bar contacts your body, lean forward and rotate the elbows upwards. 

4. Push yourself upwards away from the bar, until your arms are fully locked and you are over top of the bar.

exercises.name: Band Bent Over Row
exercises.description:
1. Start by standing on the band with both feet planted roughly shoulder-width apart. 

2. Reach your hips backwards, with knees slightly bent, while maintaining a long spine position. 

3. Fully extend your arms towards the floor. Pull your hands back towards your body, opening the chest and bringing hands towards the hip crease. Repeat.

exercises.name: Band Bicep Curl
exercises.description:
1) Stand with both feet on resistance band holding handles long next to your sides 

2) Slowly both hands up towards your shoulders, squeezing the biceps and keeping your elbows next to your sides. 

3) Slowly lower the arms back down towards the start position, then repeat.

exercises.name: Band Calf Raise
exercises.description:
1) Grab an exercise band and stand on it with your toes making sure that the length of the band between the foot and the arms is the same for both sides. 

2) While holding the handles of the band, raise the arms to the side of your head as if you were getting ready to perform a shoulder press. 

3) The palms should be facing forward with the elbows bent and to the sides. 

4) This movement will create tension on the band. This will be your starting position. 

5) Keeping the hands by your shoulder, stand up on your toes as you exhale and contract the calves hard at the top of the movement. 

6) After a one second contraction, slowly go back down to the starting position. 

7) Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.

exercises.name: Band Deadlift
exercises.description:
1. Start by standing on the band with both feet set about shoulder-width apart.

2. With the ends of the band in both hands, reach your hips back with slightly bent knees and a long spine position. 

3. Drive your hips forward to return to the start position and repeat.

exercises.name: Band External Shoulder Rotation
exercises.description:
1. Start by securing a band to an anchor point at hip's height. 

2. Stand with the handle in one hand, and your body parallel to the band. 

3. Bring the hand of the banded arm up so that your elbow is bent to 90 degrees. 

4. Slowly push the band outwards and away from your body, and the anchor point. 

5. Return to the start position. Repeat on the opposite side.

exercises.name: Band External Shoulder Rotation (90 degrees)
exercises.description:
1. Start by securing a band to an anchor point at chest height. 

2. Stand with the handle in one hand, and facing the anchor point. 

3. Bring the arm of the banded hand up so that your upper arm is parallel to the floor, and elbow bent to 90 degrees. 

4. Point your hand towards the anchor point, then slowly push the band backwards and away from the anchor point. 

5. Return to the start position. Repeat on the opposite side.

exercises.name: Band Face Pulls
exercises.description:
1. Start by securing a band to an anchor point above head height. 

2. With the handles in both hands, pull the band up and towards your temples.

3. Your upper arms should be parallel to the floor, with your hands pointed towards the ceiling. 

4. Slowly return to the start position. Repeat.

exercises.name: Band Front To Lateral Raise
exercises.alternateName: band front to side raise, band front raise opener
exercises.description:
Perform a front raise with one arm, then switch to a lateral raise. 

Alternate the movements between the front and side raises while maintaining control of the band.

exercises.name: Band Glute Kickback
exercises.description:
1. Start by securing a band to an anchor at foot height.

2. Connect the band to your foot with an ankle strap, and use the anchor for balance. 

3. Slightly hinge forward at the hips, and drive the banded leg back and away from the anchor point.

4. Slowly return to the start position. Repeat on the opposite side.

exercises.name: Band Internal Shoulder Rotation
exercises.description:
1. Start by securing a band to an anchor point at about hip height. 

2. Stand with the handle in one hand, and your body perpendicular the anchor point. Hold the band in the hand closest to the anchor point. 

3. Bring the arm of the banded hand up so that your elbow is bent to 90 degrees. 

4. Point your hand straight forward, then slowly push the band inwards and away from the anchor point. 

5. Return to the start position. Repeat on the opposite side.

exercises.name: Band Internal Shoulder Rotation (90 degrees)
exercises.description:
1. Start by securing a band to an anchor point at chest height. 

2. Stand with the handle in one hand, and facing away from the anchor point. 

3. Bring the arm of the banded hand up so that your upper arm is parallel to the floor, and elbow bent to 90 degrees. 

4. Point your hand towards the ceiling, then slowly push the band forwards and away from the anchor point. 

5. Return to the start position. Repeat on the opposite side.

exercises.name: Band Lat Pull Down
exercises.description:
1. Start by securing a band to an anchor that is above head height. 

2. From a kneeling position, grasp the band with both hands. 

3. Fully extend your arms up towards the anchor point, until your elbows are straight. 

4. Pull the band all the way down until the handles are next to your shoulders. Repeat.

exercises.name: Band Lateral Raise
exercises.description: Stand on a resistance band and hold the handles with both hands. Raise your arms out to the sides until they are parallel to the floor, keeping a slight bend in the elbows, then slowly lower back down.

exercises.name: Band Lunge to Curl
exercises.description:
Stand with your forward foot on a superband, holding the band handles with both hands at your sides. 

Step back into a reverse lunge, keeping your chest upright and core engaged. While in the lunge position, perform a bicep curl by bringing the band toward your shoulders. 


Lower your hands back to the starting position, push through your front heel to return to standing, and repeat.

exercises.name: Band Lying Leg Press
exercises.description:
1) Lay on the floor with your feet off the floor and knees bent. Place the resistance band around your feet and grab it with your hands. This will be your starting position. 

2) Execute the movement by pushing your feet out away from your body until the legs are fully extended.

3) Return to the start position and repeat.

exercises.name: Band Overhead Reverse Lunge
exercises.description:
1. Start by standing with feet together, on the band, and the band in both hands. 

2. Press the band overhead. While maintaining this position, step one foot back into a lunge position. 

3. Push yourself up to return to the start position. Repeat on the other side.

exercises.name: Band Overhead Tricep Extension
exercises.description:
1. Start by standing on the band with feet shoulder-width apart. 

2. Take the band in both hands, press it directly overhead, then bend the elbows so that the hands come towards the upper back.

3. While keeping the elbows in this position, extend the hands upwards towards the ceiling until your arms are straight. Return to the start position under control and repeat.

exercises.name: Band Pallof Press
exercises.description:
1. Start by securing a band to an anchor point at about chest height. 

2. Stand facing perpendicular to the band, holding the handles in both hands. 

3. Place your feet slightly wider than shoulder-width, with knees bent. 

4. Press the handles directly outwards and away from your body without letting your torso rotate. 

5. Return to the start position, and repeat on the opposite side.

exercises.name: Band Pallof Press and Raise
exercises.description:
1. Start by securing a band to an anchor point at about chest height. 

2. Stand facing perpendicular to the band, holding the handle in both hands. 

3. Place your feet slightly wider than shoulder-width, with knees bent. 

4. Press the handle directly outwards and away from your body without letting your torso rotate. With arms extended, bring the handle directly overhead.

5. Slowly return to the start position, and repeat on the opposite side.

exercises.name: Band Plank Row
exercises.description:
1. Start by securing a band to an anchor point at foot height. 

2. Get into a plank position on your palms.

3. With the band in one hand, reach towards the anchor point until your arm is completely straight.

4. Pull the band all the way in towards your ribs. 

5. Slowly return to the start position. Repeat on the opposite side.

exercises.name: Band Pull Aparts
exercises.description: Band Pull Aparts

exercises.name: Band Pull Through
exercises.description:
1. Start by securing a band to an anchor point at foot height. 

2. While facing away from the anchor point, ensure that the band goes in between your legs. Stand with feet shoulder-width apart.

3. Holding the handles, hinge at the hips, bringing the torso forwards and the hands back towards the anchor. 

4. Drive your hips forward to return to the standing start position.

exercises.name: Band Reverse Lunge
exercises.description:
1. Start by standing with feet together, on the band, and the band in both hands at shoulder height.

2. While maintaining this position, step one foot back into a lunge position.

3. Push yourself up to return to the start position. Repeat on the other side.

exercises.name: Band Shoulder Press
exercises.description:
1) Stand with both feet on the center of the band, hip-width apart. 

2) Bring handles of the band above shoulders so that elbows are bent 90 degrees. 

3) Press arms straight up until elbows are lock. Slowly lower to the start position and repeat.

exercises.name: Band Single Arm Bicep Curl
exercises.description:
1) Stand with both feet on resistance band holding handles next to your sides 

2) Slowly curl one hand up to your shoulder, squeezing the bicep and keeping your elbow next to your side.

3) Slowly lower your arm back down to starting position. Repeat all your reps on this side before switching to the opposite side.

exercises.name: Band Single Arm Decline Chest Press
exercises.description:
1. Start by securing a band to an anchor point above head height, and standing in front of it. 

2. Grasp the handles in both hands, with your hands up by your shoulders, in a "pushup" position. Slightly lean your torso forward.

3. With one foot forward, and one back, press one of your hands outwards and downwards until your arm is fully extended.

4. Return to the start position under control. Repeat on the opposite side.

exercises.name: Band Single Arm Kneeling Overhead Tricep Extension
exercises.description:
1. Start by securing a band to an anchor point at foot height. 

2. Grasp the band in one hand and bring your arm up overhead, so your elbow is near your ears. 

3. Bend at the elbow, bringing your hand toward your upper back.

4. Extend your hand up towards the ceiling, while keeping your elbow in place. 

5. Slowly return to the start position. Repeat on the opposite side.

exercises.name: Band Single Arm Lat Pull Down
exercises.description:
1. Start by securing a band to an anchor that is above head height. 

2. From a kneeling position, grasp the band with one hand. 

3. Fully extend your arm up towards the anchor point, until your elbow is straight. 

4. Pull the band all the way down until the handle is next to your shoulders. Repeat on the opposite side.

exercises.name: Band Single Arm Shoulder Adduction
exercises.description:
1. Start by securing a band to an anchor point at head height or above. 

2. Stand with the band at your side, holding it with a single arm. 

3. With your arm parallel to the ground, pull the band until it is down by the side of your thigh.

4. Return to the start position, and repeat on the opposite side.

exercises.name: Band Single Arm Straight Arm Pulldown
exercises.description:
1. Start by securing a band to an anchor point above head height. 

2. With the handles in both hands, stand facing the anchor point. 

3. Holding one hand down by your side, reach the other arm straight up towards the anchor point.

4. Bring the banded arm down towards your side without bending the elbow. 

4. Repeat on the opposite side.

exercises.name: Band Single Leg Lying Press
exercises.description:
1) Lay on the floor with your feet flat and knees bent. Raise one leg off of the ground. Place the resistance band around your foot and grab it with your hands. This will be your starting position. 

2) Execute the movement by pushing your foot out away from your body until the leg is fully extended.

3) Return to the start position and repeat.

exercises.name: Band Split Squat
exercises.description:
1. Start by standing on the band, with feet together, while holding it in both hands at chest height. 

2. Step one foot back and plant the toes on the ground. 

3. Slowly drop the back knee towards the floor, then push back up to the starting position without stepping together. 

4. Repeat on the opposite side.

exercises.name: Band Standing Hip Adduction
exercises.description:
1. Start by securing a band to an anchor at foot height.

2. Connect the band to your foot with an ankle strap.

3. Stand facing perpendicular to the anchor point, with the banded leg on the inside. Reach the banded leg towards the anchor point.

4. Return to the start position. Repeat on the opposite side.
